title: cerastium glutinosum subsp. pallens
intro: Cerastium glutinosum subsp. pallens, also known as the Snowdon white or Welsh snow-avens, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to the mountains of Wales and the Lake District in England. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and ease pain. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects that can help to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support overall health and well-being. In herbal preparations, Cerastium glutinosum subsp. pallens is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties, and it has been used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, digestive problems, and respiratory infections.
